seoTitle: Point Lowly vs Point Pearce — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Lowly and Point Pearce. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Lowly vs Point Pearce: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Lowly and Point Pearce.
heading: Point Lowly vs Point Pearce — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Lowly, South Australia and Point Pearce, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Pearce has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Point Lowly (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Lowly or Point Pearce safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Lowly has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Point Pearce scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Lowly scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Point Pearce scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Lowly or Point Pearce?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Pearce has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
